They don’t even think about you at all.

They can’t even comprehend the pain they cause. It’s not even their fault, our lives are just so far from their lived experience. The truth is almost everyone of any party who makes it into politics is unlikely to have faced true hopelessness even for a few weeks.

We judge a country on GDP, inflation, employment and the price of petrol. What does any of that really mean? Those easy numbers bundle so many complex factors together that the whole becomes meaningless. It’s like judging how beautiful a garden is by how much it weighs.

I want a world where we judge a country on the number of children who will be lucky to get dinner, let alone a Christmas dinner this year. Or the number of pensioners who’ll wet themselves to avoid moving from the warmth they’ve created under every blanket they own this winter.
Those examples are extreme, but it doesn’t make them any less true.

But how about something more relatable, like the number of people who help a neighbour or stranger without expectation of reward or return. Or the number of people who feel confident about their children’s ability to buy a home and live there without fear.

Imagine a government held to account under those metrics, imagine a country that forced a government to make every decision after at least considering how it will affect the people they can never really understand - but should still care about.
The true joke is, even if we do decide to keep judging a country on GDP, inflation, employment and the price of petrol, the Tories have failed us utterly.

When I lost you I finally understood why people write love songs. From the Greeks to the greats. Every word penned made sense to me.

It's so easy to write about love. We've been practicing for countless generations. All the similies and metaphors have been used before. Codified and defined. A box of Chocolates and a bouquet of roses. Butterflies and bottomless eyes.

Nobody has done the other end. The very end and everything after. We're struck speechless in the face of true eternity. Words fail us, heads empty. Unless you want empty platitudes and the same old rehearsed verses.
It's too big, too painful and too permanent.
People say love lasts forever, that's just a distraction from our true destination.

We need to work out a way to turn old people into batteries.
We've got loads of them and they contribute very little to society.
They are actually one of the biggest drains on our economy.

If they could be better utilised to power part of our grid it'd lessen our reliance on non-renewable energy. This isn't a new idea, think of it as fossil fuels without all that annoying waiting around for millions of years.
